Got it — I traced this all the way down. Here's why the transcripts don't go back to your first sessions on this project.

## The short version

`episodes/transcripts/` is **not a session archive**. It's a byproduct of episode cards, which are themselves a byproduct of *capture producing a fact*. So a transcript only exists for a session that (a) lives in the `~/.claude` folder for *this exact directory*, and (b) actually generated a card. Three separate filters sit between "a session you had" and "a transcript on disk."

## What the data actually shows

**1. The oldest transcript is `2026-05-28` — and that's also the day this repo was born.**
- First git commit: `2026-05-28 23:22 "Initial commit: proactive-context v0.1 + v0.2 capture/inject spec"`
- Oldest raw session log in `~/.claude/projects/-Users-pablofernandez-src-proactive-context/` is `b3c7dfbe…jsonl`, **also May 28** (21:13).

So inside *this working directory's* `~/.claude` folder, there is **nothing before May 28**. The archeologist can only replay JSONL that exists in `~/.claude` — it cannot mine sessions the logs don't contain. The oldest transcript matching the oldest raw log isn't a coincidence; it's the floor.

**2. Your earlier "writing about this project" sessions are under different `~/.claude` keys.** The archeologist groups transcripts by `normalize_path(cwd)` (`src/archeologist.rs:8`). Claude Code stores logs per *working directory*, and you have proactive-context conversations under at least two other keys:
- `-Users-pablofernandez-Work-proactive-context` — a **second checkout** at `~/Work/proactive-context` (separate sessions, ~Jun 3)
- `-Users-pablofernandez` (home dir, May 28) and `-Users-pablofernandez-src-claude-history` (May 20) — likely where early brainstorming happened before the repo existed

None of these normalize to `src/proactive-context`, so capturing *this* wiki never touches them. Any design/spec conversations you had before `mkdir src/proactive-context && git init` are sitting under one of those other keys (or have aged out of `~/.claude` entirely via Claude Code's own log retention).

**3. Even within range, transcripts are sparse by design — and one known bug dropped sessions.**
- One transcript per **card**, not per session — sessions that produced no captured fact produce no card and no transcript. That's why the date histogram clusters on archeologist *run* days (35 on 05-29, 16 on 06-06, 18 on 06-17) rather than spreading evenly.
- The `mark_captured`-runs-unconditionally-on-timeout bug silently marked timed-out sessions as captured with zero output, permanently excluding them. Recovery is `pc archeologist reset` then re-run.

## If you want the early sessions back

They're recoverable *if* the JSONL still exists. Point the archeologist at the other directory keys:

```bash
pc archeologist --project /Users/pablofernandez/Work/proactive-context
pc archeologist --project /Users/pablofernandez   # home-dir brainstorming sessions
```

Want me to scan those alternate keys and report what early proactive-context conversations they actually contain (dates + first user message), so you can see exactly what's missing and decide whether to fold them into this wiki?

Done — I read all 28 substantial no-card sessions (the other 4 are 2–3 line `/login`, `/model`, and slash-command stubs with no content). Here's the honest verdict.

## Mostly yes, the pipeline was right — but my earlier framing was wrong

"No episode card" does **not** mean "knowledge was lost." The no-card sessions break down as:

| Category | Count (approx) | Worth preserving? |
|---|---|---|
| Read-only Q&A ("how does inject work?", "what data points do we inject?") | ~11 | No — Pablo consuming the design, not shaping it |
| Routine git/branch/worktree ops | 3 | No — point-in-time, branches now landed |
| Mechanical edits (colorize output, batch-size fix, installs) | ~5 | No design rationale |
| Slash-command / API-errored / interrupted stubs | ~6 | No content at all |
| **Captured as a *guide*, not a card** | 2 | **Yes — and already preserved** |
| Genuinely uncaptured durable knowledge | — | **2 small findings** |

The most important correction to what I implied earlier: two of these sessions (**TENEX ingestion** `94d06a3c`, **branch-management** `0ce97719`) had their knowledge routed into **guides** (`tenex-ingestion.md`, `branch-management.md`) that *explicitly cite those session IDs*. So "no episode card" was hiding successful capture, not loss. The sparse transcript folder is mostly the pipeline working correctly, not the silent-drop bug.

## The 2 genuinely-uncaptured items (both from the foundational `1fe0f5c6` session)

1. **Inject has no transcript-triage gate** — its fast model is a wiki *navigator*, not a "should we even look?" gate. This is a deliberate architectural *asymmetry* vs. capture (which does have a triage pre-check). Not stated anywhere in the wiki. ⚠️ Reflects 2026-05-29 code — needs a sanity-check against current `inject.rs` before capture.

2. **Claude Code's statusline is display-only** — no focus/click/modal is possible via the statusLine API. A hard platform constraint that bounds statusline UX (prevents re-litigating "make it interactive"). Absent from the wiki.

A third, marginal one — the ONNX unbounded-batch → `batch_size: Some(32)` memory fix — is a magic-number fix already generalized by the 2026-06-14 memory-pressure episodes. Below the bar.

## And on your original concern

The oldest surviving session (`b3c7dfbe`, 2026-05-28) is **not** original project framing — it's a one-off "is Cohere rerank worth integrating?" question. So the early "first sessions writing about this project" you remember genuinely are **not** in `~/.claude` for this directory. They're either under the `~/Work/proactive-context` checkout (verified to hold real pc work) or aged out entirely.

Locked: **the probe → research/topic note; the cited answer → entity/definition bucket.** That's the complete split.

Here's the staged plan. I'm sequencing by risk because these prompts gate *every* capture, and your own history says capture-prompt changes get validated before landing (the spin/eval harness, and "over-splitting feeds the ROUTE bottleneck").

## Stage 1 — Micro/cosmetic decisions (low risk, clear win)
Pure prompt surgery, no new data paths:
- **EXTRACT_PREAMBLE** (capture.rs:622): add a cosmetic worked example pair + a scope rule — *"Visual, cosmetic, copy, ordering, and default-value choices are product spec; capture them with the same weight as functional behavior. A decision's value is independent of whether the user explained it."*
- Re-scope the `Skip transient one-off debugging` line (:654) so it can't be read as "small change → skip."
- **Triage** (:336): add that a UI/UX/output-format change is never "transient operational."

This *raises* volume on purpose — which you've already ratified (tokens cheap, dropped nuance expensive). Low blast radius because it only widens what counts as a spec; it doesn't touch routing or supersession.

## Stage 2 — Entity/definition bucket (the cited answer)
This is the already-recognized gap (`capture-pipeline.md:40`): EXTRACT's taxonomy has no slot for "X is Y." Add an entity-claim type sourced from in-session *investigation* statements, transcript-cited and verbatim-sliceable so the trust invariant holds. This is the part that captures the *answers* in your Q&A sessions. Higher risk — it feeds ROUTE, the known bottleneck — so it lands behind the eval harness.

## Stage 3 — Inquiry/attention → research seed (the act of probing)
When a session is question-dominated with no spec delta, instead of emitting `[]`, route the probed topic into the existing `capture_research` / `docs/wiki/research/` path as a research/topic seed. This captures *that you cared about X*, separate from the answer.
